title: Parosela frutescens
taxonomy: Parosela frutescens is classified as a species within the family Melastomataceae, commonly known as the melastome family. This family is a group of flowering plants native to tropical regions around the world. The family Melastomataceae is part of the order Myrtales, which also includes other flowering plant families such as the myrtle family and the staphylocarpus family. The order Myrtales is within the clade rosids, a large group of flowering plants. The clade rosids is a subset of the larger clade of flowering plants known as the eudicots.
uses: Parosela frutescens is known to be used in traditional medicine. Its extracts have been found to exhibit antimicrobial and antioxidant properties. In some cultures, it is used to treat fever, cough, and respiratory issues. Additionally, it is believed to possess anti-inflammatory properties, making it a potential remedy for various health conditions. Further research is needed to fully understand its medicinal applications and benefits.
